Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: This JSON file defines the structured outline of the Data Visualization with Python module, listing all topics and subtopics—including Matplotlib basics, line and scatter plots, bar charts, histograms, subplots, figure styling, and Pandas data handling—serving as the navigational backbone instructors use to organize and sequence the module's content.

Artifacts.Name: Descriptive Statistics with Pandas
Artifacts.Description: This reading introduces learners to Pandas descriptive statistics methods, covering how to compute summary measures such as mean, median, standard deviation, and aggregations on DataFrames, enabling learners to quickly explore and summarize engineering and scientific datasets without writing statistical formulas from scratch.

Artifacts.Name: Introduction to Matplotlib and the pyplot Interface
Artifacts.Description: This reading introduces learners to the Matplotlib library and its pyplot interface, covering what Matplotlib is, how pyplot works, and the distinction between Figure and Axes objects, giving learners the foundational conceptual framework needed before creating any plots in Python.

Artifacts.Name: Line Plots and Scatter Plots
Artifacts.Description: This reading teaches learners how to create and customize line plots and scatter plots using Matplotlib, explaining when each plot type is appropriate, how to control markers and line styles, and how to use them to visualize trends or relationships in scientific and engineering data.

Artifacts.Name: Bar Charts and Histograms
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains the differences between bar charts and histograms and teaches learners how to create, annotate, and interpret both using Matplotlib, illustrating when to use each for comparing categorical quantities versus visualizing the distribution of continuous data.

Artifacts.Name: Subplots and Figure Layout
Artifacts.Description: This reading teaches learners how to arrange multiple plots within a single Matplotlib figure using plt.subplots(), covering how to create subplot grids, plot on individual Axes objects, and control figure layout to enable side-by-side comparison of related visualizations.

Artifacts.Name: Publication-Quality Figure Styling
Artifacts.Description: This reading guides learners through the techniques required to style Matplotlib figures to publication or professional standards, covering typography, color choices, line weights, tick formatting, and other visual elements needed for journal articles, reports, or conference posters.

Artifacts.Name: Introduction to Pandas: Series and DataFrames
Artifacts.Description: This reading introduces learners to the two core Pandas data structures—Series and DataFrames—explaining their properties, how they are created, and how they relate to NumPy, giving learners the foundational knowledge needed for all subsequent data manipulation and analysis tasks.

Artifacts.Name: Reading, Filtering, and Sorting Data with Pandas
Artifacts.Description: This reading teaches learners how to load structured data into Pandas DataFrames from files, and how to filter rows and sort data using Pandas' expressive querying and indexing tools, enabling learners to prepare and slice real datasets for analysis.

Artifacts.Name: Descriptive Statistics with Pandas
Artifacts.Description: This reading introduces learners to Pandas descriptive statistics methods, covering how to compute summary measures such as mean, median, standard deviation, and aggregations on DataFrames, enabling learners to quickly explore and summarize engineering and scientific datasets without writing statistical formulas from scratch.
